title: Vaccinium poianum
intro: Vaccinium poayanum, commonly known as the Andean blueberry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). It is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to promote cardiovascular health, boost immune function, and support healthy digestion. The bioactive constituents of Vaccinium poayanum include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Vaccinium poayanum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are valued for their potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and support overall well-being.
